name: Python assignment
instructions:
STEPS
What is a Program?



A program is a set of instructions that a computer follows to complete a task. The computer receives the information, handles it, utilizes it, and then gives an output based on it by following a program.We are surrounded by computers and computer programs; phones or tablets you can play or watch videos on. Other example are traffic lights and they follow different programs to carry out different tasks.



Let's See How Programs Work!

When you want to play your favorite game, like "Minecraft" or "Roblox," you tap its picture, right? A special program inside the tablet quickly makes the game start for you! It's like the tablet knows exactly how to open the game because of those instructions.



Another example is that when you're in a car, you see traffic lights turn red, then yellow, then green. They don't just change randomly! A program inside the traffic light tells it exactly when to turn red (so cars stop) and when to turn green (so cars go).



According to Linda Liukas, a Finnish computer programmer, children’s writer, and programming instructor, programming consists of three parts:Planning the programWriting the program in a programming language into a codeTesting and debugging the program







The second part is what everybody generally considers programming. Only this part requires coding skills; the first and third parts require additional skills like problem-solving, and decomposition (the ability to break down bigger problems into smaller, manageable tasks).



Programming AnalogyGetting Dressed

On days that you have to go to school, your mother and father are behind you in the morning, asking you to wake up, while you keep saying ‘5 more minutes!’ until you realize it is late and you don’t have time to take bath. So, you decide to just wash your face, brush your teeth, and get dressed. To get dressed, you follow a particular sequence of steps, like the one given below:
